candidates.name: Mikie Sherrill
candidates.notes: First female military veteran elected governor of any US state. Navy helicopter pilot who flew missions in Europe and Middle East. Federal prosecutor who handled narcotics and organized crime cases. Heritage Action lifetime 2%. NRA F grade. Sworn in on Constitution rather than Bible — drew criticism from religious conservatives. Won governor's race by 14 points in what was expected to be competitive. Represents new model of national-security Democrat.

profile.religion: Roman Catholic (sworn in on Constitution, not Bible)
profile.education: B.S. United States Naval Academy (1994); M.Sc. International & World History, London School of Economics (2003); J.D. Georgetown University Law Center
profile.background: Navy helicopter pilot and federal prosecutor; U.S. Representative (2019-2025); became New Jersey's 57th governor and first female military veteran elected governor in January 2026.

candidates.name: Cory Booker
candidates.notes: Heritage Action lifetime 7%. NRA F grade — proposed federal gun licensing. Set record for longest Senate floor speech (25 hours, 5 minutes) on March 31, 2025. Vegan. Rhodes Scholar. Deeply interfaith — Baptist who was co-president of Oxford Chabad House; says 'Christ is the center of my life' but quotes Torah extensively. His faith framing is progressive: love, poverty, stranger-welcoming rather than cultural conservatism. 2020 presidential candidate. Single — no spouse or children.

profile.religion: Baptist (deeply interfaith — co-president of Oxford Chabad House; quotes Torah, quotes Jesus)
profile.education: B.A. Political Science, Stanford University (1991); M.A. Sociology, Stanford (1992); Rhodes Scholar, Oxford University (1994); J.D. Yale Law School (1997)
profile.background: Former Mayor of Newark, New Jersey. Rhodes Scholar. Community organizer and attorney. Ran for president in 2020. Known for work on criminal justice reform.
profile.office: 306 Hart Senate Office Building

candidates.name: Analilia Mejia
candidates.notes: Sworn in April 20, 2026 after special election to fill the seat vacated by Mikie Sherrill (now NJ Governor). Former national political director for Bernie Sanders's 2020 presidential campaign; deputy director of the U.S. Women's Bureau under Biden. Co-executive director of the Center for Popular Democracy. Public positions: Medicare for All, $25/hr minimum wage, abolition of ICE, tuition-free public college, expansion of labor protections. Defeated Joe Hathaway (R) 59.76% to 40.24%.

profile.education: Rutgers University–New Brunswick (BA Comparative Literature, 2000; MPP 2002; MA Labor Relations 2003)
profile.background: National political director for Bernie Sanders's 2020 presidential campaign. Deputy Director, U.S. Department of Labor Women's Bureau (2021–2023). Co-executive director, Center for Popular Democracy. Sworn in to U.S. House April 20, 2026.
